title: websave
text: websave Writes a JSON stream from the data in a component to $webinfo("data") . websave{/mod | /one} Copy Example: websave Qualifiers Table 1. Qualifiers Qualifier Description /mod Includes only modified occurrences in the JSON stream. /one Includes only current outer occurrence (with all inner occurrences) in the JSON stream. Return Values The output of websave includes the CRC, ID, and STATUS for every occurrence and is put in the $webinfo ("data") channel. For more information, see Metadata for Reconnect . Table 2. Values returned in $status Value Meaning <0 An error occurred. $procerror contains the exact error. 0 Success >0 Number of images for which a temporary file could not be created. Use Use in Dynamic Server Page components. Description The websave statement creates a JSON stream from the data in a component. The stream is built from the complete hitlist, including occurrences currently marked for deletion. Occurrences and fields are selected from the data set based on switches used by the websave statement. The entities and fields included in the stream are defined by the component structure, so both database and non-database fields are included in the JSON stream. You can use the $JSON_INDENT and $JSON_SHOWNAMES assignment settings to make the JSON stream more human-readable for development and debugging purposes. Triggers Fired by websave The following triggers are fired by the websave statement and can be used to customize the process of saving a component into an JSON stream: preSerialize—fired immediately before an occurrence is saved from a component into a disconnected record set. The occurrence is available and can be examined. For example, an occurrence can be excluded from the save, or the value for a derived field can be calculated. See the "trigger preSerialize topic". postSerialize—fired immediately after an occurrence is saved from a component into a disconnected record set. See the "trigger postSerialize topic". Images When the component contains images from a database, the Uniface Server creates temporary image files in the project directory. If it does not have write privileges for the project directory, this action fails. The page is still presented to the browser, but with image load error icons in the locations of the images. Example: websave The following code creates a JSON stream from a component's data structure and writes the result to the message frame. Explain this code clear retrieve/e "ORDER.INOUTER" websave putmess $webinfo("data") return Copy Related Links trigger preSerialize System trigger that processes an occurrence before it is saved from a component into a disconnected record set (XML, JSON, or Struct). trigger postSerialize System trigger that processes an occurrence after it is saved from a component into a disconnected record set (XML, JSON, or Struct). JSON The JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a data-interchange format based on a subset of JavaScript. It provides an alternative to XML and is often used for serializing and transmitting structured data over a network connection, especially in web applications. $JSON_INDENT Format JSON output with indents to make it more readable. $JSON_SHOWNAMES Show fully qualified names in JSON streams as extra attributes of fields and entities. $webinfo Return or set information about the current HTTP request or response.
